content: Just as English language learners solidify new vocabulary and writing skills through repeated reflective practice that connects new words to their own existing contextual knowledge, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the structured pedagogical reflection framework from high school ELA classrooms to improve their ability to ground abstract reasoning outputs in consistent, usable contextual memory. This framing turns a technical AI optimization problem into a question of structured skill-building practice that mirrors how humans learn through deliberate reflection.
